artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one substantive ADD teaching guidance page on AI tools and one linked SICUZ software-license page. Research/repository results were recorded and rejected rather than used as policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Uso docente de herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial | Anillo Digital Docente (ADD)
Licencias de software para estudiantes | Servicio de Informática y Comunicaciones

artifacts.claimText: Universidad de Zaragoza's ADD guidance frames AI applications as teaching tools for personalized learning and support for teachers creating teaching materials, while noting that students also use them and need to be shown correct use.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI applications are described as supporting teaching personalization, teacher material creation, and student-use guidance.

artifacts.claimText: The ADD guidance identifies several ADD-associated tools or services that use AI, including Google Workspace tools Gemini and NotebookLM, Wooclap, Kahoot, Compilatio, Genially, and Microsoft Copilot.
artifacts.normalizedValue: ADD AI-capable tools listed: Gemini, NotebookLM, Wooclap, Kahoot, Compilatio, Genially, Microsoft Copilot.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Uso docente de herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial | Anillo Digital Docente (ADD)
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Las aplicaciones que hacen uso de la Inteligencia Artificial están muy presentes en la enseñanza con objetivos como la personalización del aprendizaje o el apoyo al profesorado en la creación de materiales docentes. También son utilizadas por los estudiantes, por lo que es necesario mostrarles como se deben usar correctamente.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI applications are described as present in teaching for personalized learning and teacher support; the page adds that students use them and need to be shown correct use.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section: Uso docente de herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Uso docente de herramientas de Inteligencia Artificial | Anillo Digital Docente (ADD)
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Formando parte del conjunto de aplicaciones incluidas en Google Workspace se incluye el bot conversacional de inteligencia artificial multimodal y generativa Gemini y el asistente de investigación y escritura potenciado por inteligencia artificial NotebookLM... La herramienta Wooclap incluye una funcionalidad de creación de cuestionarios utilizando Inteligencia Artificial.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page lists Gemini and NotebookLM within Google Workspace and says Wooclap includes an AI-based quiz-creation function; nearby sections also name Kahoot, Compilatio, Genially, and Microsoft Copilot.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section: ¿QUÉ HERRAMIENTAS DEL ANILLO DIGITAL DOCENTE HACEN USO DE LA INTELIGENCIA ARTIFICIAL?

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow teaching-guidance claim; does not assert a binding university-wide AI policy.
Approved as a list of AI-capable ADD-associated tools named by the official ADD guidance, not as a mandate to use them.
Approved as a descriptive claim about Compilatio Magister+ detection functionality only.
Approved as contextual software availability evidence only; it should not be read as an AI-specific policy requirement.

artifacts.title: Universidad de Zaragoza AI Guidance Snapshot
artifacts.summary: Official Universidad de Zaragoza sources show ADD teaching guidance on AI applications, named AI-capable educational tools, Compilatio Magister+ AI-generated text detection, and contextual SICUZ Microsoft 365 Education availability. The staged claims are descriptive and do not assert a binding university-wide AI policy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No binding university-wide AI policy was found in the official sources reviewed; claims are limited to the cited ADD and SICUZ pages.
Repository and teaching-experience results were rejected as insufficient policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
